macro_summary: Big Tech earnings day delivered a split verdict: per Dow Jones Futures reporting, Google rose post-earnings while Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta slumped, pulling index futures lower overnight. The dominant macro shock is WTI crude surging +9.39% to $108.67 — the Hormuz crisis, which had seemed to stabilise near $99, has re-escalated sharply per Al Jazeera and Discovery Alert reporting, with Iran rowing back on its Strait reopening after the US maintained its naval blockade of Iranian ports. VIX rose 5.5% to 18.81 on the combined earnings disappointments and oil spike. The Employment Cost Index for Q1 is scheduled for release today at 08:30 ET, and the FOMC rate decision remains on deck for May 7 — per Yahoo Finance, Fed rate cut expectations have been pushed back to late 2026, with the Fed's preferred PCE inflation gauge now projected at 3.7% for Q2 2026, well above the 2% target.
commodities_note: WTI's +9.39% single-session surge to $108.67 is the sharpest oil move in this tracking period, breaking decisively above the $100 ceiling that had held since the ceasefire. Gold pulled back 0.74% to $4,575 — a modest giveback from recent highs but still historically elevated. The DXY edged up 0.31% to 98.98, reflecting modest dollar strength on the oil/inflation combination. The WTI spike is a direct headwind for Singapore banks (fuel inflation reinflates NIM compression pressure) and a fresh extension block for energy-linked names.

writeup.headline: Hard-blocked to CAUTION — RSI surged to 68.3 on DBS earnings day.
writeup.what_to_do: DBS reports today (earnings_calendar shows days_until=0). Yesterday's close was SGD 56.63 with RSI 46.3 near the 50-day average; today the stock surged +3.41% to SGD 58.59, pushing RSI to 68.3 and triggering the hard block. The R:R is distorted — upside to SGD 59.15 resistance is only 1.0% vs 3.8% downside to the 50-day average, giving a 0.3:1 ratio that is not actionable. Reassess after earnings: upgrade to WATCH if price consolidates below SGD 58 with RSI cooling toward 55 and NIM guidance stabilises above 4.20%.

writeup.headline: OCBC steady near its 50-day average, earnings in 8 days is the next catalyst.
writeup.what_to_do: OCBC reports May 8 (8 days out). The stock sits 3.04% above its 50-day average with RSI at 56.1 — not yet extended, but R:R is unfavourable at 1.1:1 (3.1% upside to SGD 22.51 resistance vs 2.9% downside to SGD 21.19 SMA). NIM trajectory is the key metric: upgrade to WATCH if May 8 guidance holds NIM above the Q4 2025 level; downgrade to CAUTION if NIM guide deteriorates. The WTI surge to $108.67 reinflates logistics cost inflation, which pressures the rate-cut timeline and keeps both NIM compression channels open.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to HOLD — price reclaimed the 50-day average with RSI reset to neutral.
writeup.what_to_do: UOB reports May 7 (7 days out, active-context exception). Yesterday UOB was in CAUTION with RSI 38.2 and price 1.12% below the 50-day; today it has recovered to SGD 36.08, essentially at the 50-day average (0.29% below) with RSI reset to 45.8. Forward PE of 10x at an 18% discount to the Singapore cluster median is compelling. However, R:R is unfavourable at 0.9:1 — upside to SGD 36.89 resistance is only 2.2% vs 2.4% downside to SGD 35.23 support. Upgrade to WATCH if UOB holds above the 50-day on May 7 earnings with NIM guidance stable; the WTI spike to $108.67 is a fresh headwind for the rate-cut timeline.

writeup.headline: Intel hits all-time highs — 77% above its 50-day average, untouchable for fresh entry.
writeup.what_to_do: Per Insider Monkey, Intel rallied to a fresh all-time high on analyst optimism following its earnings beat and upbeat Q2 outlook. The 14A node has Tesla as its first external customer (confirmed April 2026), which is a genuine positive signal for the next node — but 18A still has no external foundry customers, and the foundry thesis for 18A remains unproven. FCF yield is negative (-1.74%), forward PE is 148% above the semis cluster median at 64x, and the stock is 77% above its 50-day average with RSI 86.4. No entry at any level until a meaningful consolidation occurs — the $80–90 zone is the first area worth revisiting.

writeup.headline: Cleanest setup on the list, but R:R distorted by a 1% tight stop.
writeup.what_to_do: ASML has returned to just below its 50-day average ($1,399.86) at $1,394.08, with RSI at 48 — the most technically attractive positioning on the watchlist. However, the headline R:R of 6.8:1 is distorted: it uses a tight 1.0% stop to SGD 1,379.74 support. The pipeline-computed sizing R:R using structural support at $1,311.46 (5.9% below) is only 1.2:1 — unfavourable. Wide-stop R:R is 1.15:1. ASML raised full-year revenue guidance citing AI and memory orders per Simply Wall St.; large orders from SK Hynix and Samsung are filling the book. The MATCH Act remains a legislative risk for China DUV/EUV exports — surface as a risk until signed or formally killed. TSM's delay of high-NA EUV deployment until at least 2029 (per Insider Monkey, citing TSM Deputy Co-COO Kevin Zhang) is a near-term headwind for the High-NA ramp thesis. Wait for a confirmed hold above $1,400 with volume, or a pullback to $1,311–1,379 where structural R:R improves meaningfully.

Per Dow Jones Futures, MSFT slumped post-earnings. Focus metrics: non-OpenAI Azure RPO growth and Azure ex-OpenAI commentary. The stock is 7.29% above its 50-day average ($395.61) with RSI 61.8, and remains hard-blocked. Reassess at $391–400 if post-earnings selling brings it near the 50-day.

writeup.headline: Broadcom hits $2T market cap — 17% above 50-day, no entry until pullback.
writeup.what_to_do: Per Barchart, Broadcom crossed a $2 trillion market cap milestone. Q1 AI revenue rose 106% year-over-year per Zacks, with Tomahawk 6 shipping at scale. However, at +17.27% above its 50-day average ($345.74) with RSI 64.2, the stock is hard-blocked. Note this is a blended hardware + software model (VMware recurring revenue + custom ASICs) — not a pure semi multiple. The custom ASIC thesis (Google TPU, Meta chips) is intact, but do not conflate custom ASIC wins with NVDA merchant GPU dynamics. Wait for a pullback toward $369–380 before considering entry.

writeup.headline: Lumentum surges +7.16% ahead of next week's earnings — 14% above 50-day.
writeup.what_to_do: LITE reports May 6 (6 days out — active-context exception). Northland raised its price target to $1,000 per Insider Monkey, citing accelerating AI optical connectivity demand. The stock is 14.42% above its 50-day average ($750.15) with RSI 54.5, hard-blocked. FCF yield is essentially zero (-0.03%), forward PE is 52.5x (103% premium to the semis cluster median) — the valuation is pure growth/thesis. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (March 2, 2026 NVDA IR) is the confirmed load-bearing catalyst. Earnings next week is a binary event — wait for the $800–810 zone (near structural support) post-earnings before considering entry. Volume confirmed today's +7.16% surge, which is constructive but doesn't change the entry block.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 74x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

writeup.headline: Palantir below its 50-day average — Cleveland-Cliffs deal adds industrial AI leg but valuation remains a ceiling.
writeup.what_to_do: PLTR reports May 5 (5 days out). The Cleveland-Cliffs 3-year AI deal (per GuruFocus, cited by Simply Wall St.) confirms commercial expansion into US industrial manufacturing — a meaningful diversification from the pure defense narrative. However, at 74x forward PE (176% above the BigTech cluster median), the valuation demands explicit justification. The sizing R:R using structural support at $128.51 is only 0.7:1 — unfavourable. The headline R:R of 4.2:1 is distorted by a tight 1.2% stop to $136.30. Tight stop: normal intraday volatility may trigger before the thesis is tested — use $128.51 as the mental stop for sizing. Reassess post-May 5 earnings if commercial revenue growth sustains 50%+ trajectory with the government segment holding.

writeup.headline: China suspends autonomous driving permits after Baidu outage — direct regulatory headwind for WeRide.
writeup.what_to_do: Per Bloomberg, China has suspended issuing new autonomous vehicle licenses after dozens of Baidu Apollo Go robotaxis suddenly stopped in Wuhan last month. This is a direct and material headwind for WeRide's China commercialisation timeline — the permit pipeline is the primary revenue-generating mechanism for the L4 robotaxi model. The Lenovo partnership (200,000 L4 vehicles over 5 years per Simply Wall St.) is a constructive long-term signal but requires the permit regime to reopen. WRD fell 4.47% to $7.48, now 2.62% above its 50-day average ($7.29) with RSI 46.2. ADR/China delisting risk remains a standing concern. R:R is 4.6:1 to $8.33 resistance using the 50-day as stop — but the permit suspension makes this a watch-and-wait situation until China resumes licensing. Accumulate gates blocked by the regulatory development. Reassess when China restores autonomous vehicle licensing.

singapore.summary: DBS's +3.41% surge today (RSI 68.3, hard-blocked) has created a sharp intra-cluster divergence: DBS is now the most technically extended Singapore bank while UOB has reset to neutral and OCBC sits in mild extension. The cluster remains structurally impaired by the NIM compression thesis — WTI's +9.39% single-session surge reinflates the logistics inflation narrative and pushes back the rate-cut timeline, keeping both NIM headwinds (US10Y above 4.20% and MAS S$NEER appreciation) firmly in place.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression persists Day 44
singapore.key_development: WTI spike to $108.67 reinflates sticky inflation narrative, further delaying the US10Y sub-4.20% reset condition required for NIM relief.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX recovered +1.41% to $449.99 today after yesterday's -3.58% OpenAI-driven selloff, but the index remains well below April highs and every name on the list is hard-blocked by extreme extension. The cluster divergence is stark: ASML is the only name near its 50-day average and thus approaching actionable territory, while INTC (+77%), AMD (+47%), and MU (+23%) sit at extension levels that require crash-scale corrections before re-entry. TSM's confirmed delay of high-NA EUV deployment to 2029 removes a near-term ASML revenue catalyst.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Thesis intact, price wrong across the board
semiconductors.key_development: TSM's 2029 high-NA EUV delay pushes the ASML High-NA ramp revenue recognition out by at least 2 years, though AI and memory order demand continues to fill the standard EUV book.

big_tech.summary: Earnings split tonight: Google rose while Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta fell per Dow Jones Futures reporting — a divergence that confirms the cluster is not moving as a unit. MSFT's confirmed OpenAI concentration (45% of Azure backlog per Business Insider) validates the standing thesis concern. All four names remain hard-blocked by extension, with PLTR the only name below its 50-day average and approaching a potential entry zone post-May 5 earnings.
big_tech.thesis_status: Earnings binary partially resolved — divergent outcomes
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft earnings confirmed OpenAI drives 45% of Azure cloud backlog, making non-OpenAI Azure RPO the critical organic demand signal going forward.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp
interconnected.reason: Received a matching $2B strategic investment from NVDA alongside LITE — both are co-packaged optics partners for AI datacenter interconnects; LITE's +7.16% surge and pre-earnings setup makes COHR the natural paired read-through.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's May 6 earnings confirm accelerating AI optics revenue growth exceeding 50% year-over-year, which would validate the broader optical interconnect demand thesis for COHR.

interconnected.name: ExxonMobil
interconnected.reason: WTI's +9.39% single-session surge to $108.67 on renewed Hormuz escalation is the dominant macro move today — XOM is the highest-quality direct beneficiary of sustained oil above $100.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting for watchlist addition if WTI holds above $105 for 3 consecutive sessions, signalling the Hormuz premium is structural rather than a one-day spike; use a pullback to the $110–115 range in XOM stock as the entry condition.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC designer competing directly with AVGO for hyperscaler silicon — AVGO's $2T milestone and 106% YoY AI revenue growth confirm the custom ASIC market is expanding faster than merchant GPU alternatives, which is a direct read-through for MRVL's Amazon custom chip program.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL pulls back 8-10% from current levels on any broad semis selloff, bringing it near a defined support level ahead of its next earnings print.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking backbone — directly benefits from the hyperscaler capex commentary in tonight's AMZN/MSFT/GOOG earnings; every incremental dollar of datacenter build requires Arista switching infrastructure.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AMZN and MSFT earnings transcripts are available; if both companies confirm datacenter capex acceleration (not cuts), ANET becomes a watchlist candidate on any 5-8% pullback from current levels.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz re-escalation: Iran reversed its reopening decision after the US maintained its naval blockade of Iranian ports; WTI surged +9.39% to $108.67 per Al Jazeera and Discovery Alert reporting.
Fed rate cut delay: PCE inflation projected at 3.7% for Q2 2026 per Yahoo Finance survey; rate cut now expected late 2026, compounding duration headwinds for tech multiples.
China autonomous vehicle permit suspension: Bloomberg reports China halted new AV licenses after Baidu Apollo Go outage in Wuhan, directly hitting WeRide's commercialisation timeline.

Middle East airspace closures disrupting Europe-Asia aviation corridor, with Emirates, Qatar Airways, and Indian carriers suspending or rerouting Middle East operations, adding structural logistics cost inflation.
geopolitical.market_impact: The WTI surge to $108.67 is today's dominant portfolio impact — PLTR benefits from sustained defense spending tied to the conflict, while Singapore banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) face a double headwind as oil inflation delays rate cuts and extends the NIM compression cycle. The BigTech earnings split (Google up, Amazon and Microsoft down) shows the market differentiating on earnings quality, with MSFT's OpenAI concentration disclosure adding to the complexity.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: WTI surged +9.39% to $108.67 after Iran reversed its Strait of Hormuz reopening decision when the US maintained its naval blockade; China suspended autonomous vehicle licensing after the Baidu Apollo Go Wuhan outage; Google rose while Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta fell on post-close earnings per Dow Jones Futures.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash positions; the WTI re-escalation and earnings night split argue for patience — no new entries until WTI stabilises and post-earnings prices reset to better R:R levels.
